response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has essentially no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU and AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and hardware-software co-design for deep-learning workloads — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI-adjacent work at re:cinq is purely software-layer (MCP frameworks, agentic tooling) and does not translate to the hardware optimization and system architecture skills the role demands.
strengths: Over 14 years of professional software experience, satisfying the education + experience threshold for a BS-level candidate
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering in high-throughput, low-latency contexts (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Breadth across embedded, systems-level (DLang, C/C++), and cloud-native stacks shows adaptability across abstraction layers
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or chip-level performance analysis — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, AI accelerator design, SIMD/MIMD paradigms, or deep neural network training/inference optimization
No evidence of hardware-software co-design or partnering with silicon architects, which is a primary responsibility of the position

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload experience
